items.title: That, as far as I know, is the last thing I'll write about the 2025-26 NBA season. Sixteen seasons since starting to freelance, 13 since going full-time, fourth since coming back to Y!. It's cool as hell that I still get to write about this stuff for a living. Thanks for helping
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: That, as far as I know, is the last thing I'll write about the 2025-26 NBA season. Sixteen seasons since starting to freelance, 13 since going full-time, fourth since coming back to Y!. It's cool as hell that I still get to write about this stuff for a living. Thanks for helping make that possible.

items.title: Fever built another double-digit lead (11) in 2mins, but it nearly evaporated in 36 seconds.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Fever built another double-digit lead (11) in 2mins, but it nearly evaporated in 36 seconds.

Seven straight Tempo points, including two layups, so Steph White calls timeout.

items.title: Sophie Cunningham beats the buzzer with a 3-pointer to close the first quarter, putting the Fever ahead 26-20. Missed shots at the rim and turnovers (5) kept them from being up more.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Sophie Cunningham beats the buzzer with a 3-pointer to close the first quarter, putting the Fever ahead 26-20. Missed shots at the rim and turnovers (5) kept them from being up more.

Aliyah Boston has 7/6 in 6mins.

items.title: Sophie Cunningham, keeping her three-point hearter alive from Saturday night. Heads up play with the clock in mind, and a great pass from Raven Johnson. Cunningham drills a 3 at the first quarter buzzer and the Fever lead 26-20.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt: Sophie Cunningham, keeping her three-point hearter alive from Saturday night. Heads up play with the clock in mind, and a great pass from Raven Johnson. Cunningham drills a 3 at the first quarter buzzer and the Fever lead 26-20.

items.title: Myisha Hines-Allen being attended to by athletic trainer, walking back to the locker room after take a hand to her nose. Appears to be bleeding.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Myisha Hines-Allen being attended to by athletic trainer, walking back to the locker room after take a hand to her nose. Appears to be bleeding.

Aliyah Boston returns to replace her.

Fever 3/10 inside the paint to start.

items.title: Myisha Hines-Allen got hit in the face and couldn't even make it back to defense. She ran to the Fever bench during the stoppage with her hands below her face, presumably to catch something. She goes back to the locker room in an instant.

With their Game 5 Finals-clinching road win, the Knicks tied the 1995 Rockets for the MOST road wins (9) in a single postseason in NBA history!

New York’s HISTORIC run culminates in the franchise’s third championship… and first in 53 years! 🏆
